site.title: Jason Boog
site.metaDescription: An online home for journalist Jason Boog, author of "Born Reading: Bringing Up Bookworms in a Digital Age" and "The Deep End: The Literary Scene in the Great Depression and Today."

quotes.text: Digressions, incontestably, are the sunshine — they are the life, the soul of reading!
The reader sees the world through the detective’s eyes, experiencing the proliferation of its details as if for the first time. He has become awake to the things around him.
quotes.attr: The New York Trilogy by Paul Auster

quotes.text: Trust nobody and love nothing except literature.
quotes.attr: Call Me Zebra by Azareen Van der Vliet Oloomi

quotes.text: I have not died; for I always carry a little notebook and pencil to annotate everything, and if that had happened to me, I would have written it down.
It’s the opposite of a blog. It’s an antiblog, because it’s meant for only one special person, and that person is you.
quotes.attr: A Tale for the Time Being by Ruth Ozeki

quotes.text: Whether you’re a human being, an insect, a microbe, or a stone, this verse is true. All that you touch, You Change. All that you Change, Changes you.
quotes.attr: Parable of the Sower by Octavia E. Butler

about.headline: I am an author and journalist, and I lead the editorial team at Fable.
paragraphs: My work has appeared in *The Believer*, *Salon*, *The Awl*, *NPR Books*, *The Los Angeles Review of Books*, *Peace Corps Writers*, and other outlets. I was the west coast correspondent for *Publishers Weekly*.
After graduating from the University of Michigan, I spent two years teaching youth groups in Peace Corps Guatemala.

I earned my M.A. in journalism at New York University, then worked as an investigative reporter at Judicial Reports, served as the publishing editor at Mediabistro, and led the research department at True Pictures.

entries.title: Senior Manager, Editorial
entries.description: I lead book club, home feed, and AI editorial strategy, supervising a team of 4 writers and helming projects for career coaching, corporate clubs, and university learning.

entries.title: West Coast Correspondent
entries.description: Covered publishing industry news, developments, and deals, focusing on the intersection of tech & publishing, reporting numerous features on AI & writing.

entries.title: Editorial Director
entries.description: Led content marketing strategy for clients’ articles, social posts, branding documents, presentations & fan community interactions, leading a team of 5-10 writers.

entries.title: Director, Story Research & Development
entries.description: Built a network of expert consultants, wrote research reports, and collaborated on story development. Supervised a team of 3-5 writers & researchers.

entries.title: Publishing Editor
entries.description: Edited the GalleyCat and AppNewser blogs, maintained our robust social media footprint, curated publishing curriculum and led a team of 4-6 bloggers.

entries.title: Adjunct Faculty
entries.org: New York University School of Journalism · New York, NY
entries.description: Taught introductory journalism and journalism ethics courses, introducing my students to real-life scenarios from my own work.

entries.title: Staff Writer
entries.description: Served as an investigative reporter for this judicial watchdog website, writing weekly features about judges in New York City.

entries.title: Volunteer
entries.description: Worked with youth groups on small business and creative projects. I wrote and designed a training manual for new volunteers.

entries.title: Writing on AI and the future of writing
entries.org: *Publishers Weekly* · *Why Is This Interesting?* · SuperRare · Medium
entries.description: Reporting and essays on developments in AI and what they mean for writers and publishers.

entries.title: Responsible AI workshop
entries.org: Fintech communications team · New York, NY
entries.description: Two-hour in-person training on responsible AI use for a large communications team.

entries.title: Featured speaker, U.S. Copyright Office hearing
entries.org: "Copyright in the Age of A.I.: Session 5 — AI and Creating a World of Other Works" · Washington, D.C.
